conclusion: Kushner's operating pattern is to enter messy, high-stakes situations and reduce them to incentives, counterparties, capital structure, and trust. The through-line from 666 Fifth Avenue to the Abraham Accords to Affinity Partners is deal mechanics under pressure.
background: This is one of the longest Invest Like the Best episodes, covering Kushner's New York real-estate career, the New York Observer, prison reform, Operation Warp Speed, the Abraham Accords, Middle East diplomacy, and Affinity Partners. The most useful business sections are concrete: the 666 Fifth Avenue capital-structure mistake and rescue, the value of fair dealing in competitive markets, and Affinity's attempt to combine geopolitical judgment with private-equity underwriting in markets such as Brazil and Israel.
important_facts: Kushner says he completed roughly $14B of transactions in his New York real-estate chapter. He bought 666 Fifth Avenue in 2007 for $1.8B, then the market changed after Lehman. The most useful business sections are concrete: the 666 Fifth Avenue capital-structure mistake and rescue, the value of fair dealing in competitive markets, and Affinity's attempt to combine geopolitical judgment with private-equity underwriting in markets such as Brazil and Israel.

The 666 thesis depended on retail income rising from roughly $12-15M to $50-60M, valued at 20-25x. The building was about 1.5M square feet, and the retail alone was expected to be worth roughly $1.0-1.3B after condominium separation. Later in Affinity, Kushner describes a Brazil case where rates moved from about 2% to 14%, a food-waste reduction effort using AI/cameras targeting 35%, and an Israeli Phoenix investment moving toward about 10% ownership.

Carlyle and the Chera family bought 50% of the 666 retail for $525M, helping repay debt. Later in Affinity, Kushner describes a Brazil case where rates moved from about 2% to 14%, a food-waste reduction effort using AI/cameras targeting 35%, and an Israeli Phoenix investment moving toward about 10% ownership. PHOE.TA (Long) — Kushner describes buying 4.9% of Phoenix Holdings with an option toward about 10% ownership as a blue-chip Israel dislocation trade.

mental_model.name: Mechanic's View Of Power
mental_model.components: The 666 deal was a capital-structure lesson more than a thesis failure. He says the retail thesis was directionally right, but timing and leverage were wrong. Plaza District vacancy was around 4%, rents were rising double digits, then the crash cut rents from about $120 to $60 and exposed how dangerous a concentrated bet can be without enough capital flexibility. Negotiation starts by listening for the real objective. Kushner says most people tell you what they want if you listen. In government, he was surprised how little negotiation experience many officials had, even though the stakes were far higher than a real-estate deal. Affinity is built around country-level incentives and trusted access. He looks for places where policy, capital, and private execution line up. Examples include Brazil after rates spiked, Israeli financials after war-driven dislocation, and businesses where AI or operational changes can raise margins.
mental_model.application: Kushner's operating pattern is to enter messy, high-stakes situations and reduce them to incentives, counterparties, capital structure, and trust. The through-line from 666 Fifth Avenue to the Abraham Accords to Affinity Partners is deal mechanics under pressure. Later in Affinity, Kushner describes a Brazil case where rates moved from about 2% to 14%, a food-waste reduction effort using AI/cameras targeting 35%, and an Israeli Phoenix investment moving toward about 10% ownership.

top_investment_implications.thesis: Differentiated geopolitical network and private-equity mandate may find idiosyncratic opportunities in markets other managers avoid, but outside investors have limited transparency
Kushner describes buying 4.9% of Phoenix Holdings with an option toward about 10% ownership as a blue-chip Israel dislocation trade
Affinity saw opportunity after Brazilian rates moved from roughly 2% to 14%, pressuring valuations while leaving some cash-flow businesses intact

golden_quotes: "Kushner says his mindset during the 666 Fifth Avenue crisis became, 'thank you for this training,' because the pressure forced rapid learning."
"His postmortem on 666 is that the retail thesis was right, but the timing and capital structure were wrong."
"The broader operating lesson he draws from crisis is that you learn very little when things go right."

chronology.subject: Jared Kushner business and government career

events.event: Kushner bought 666 Fifth Avenue for $1.8B before the financial crisis
Lehman crash changed New York office dynamics and pressured the 666 capital structure
Carlyle and the Chera family bought 50% of the retail for $525M, helping recapitalize the asset
Served in the Trump administration, working on prison reform, Operation Warp Speed, and Middle East policy
Abraham Accords signed after years of regional diplomacy
Founded Affinity Partners to invest across global markets using business and government experience
